title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carlolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, from Bristol, R.I., were killed in a Swansea crash on Route 136 and Route 6.
Suspect Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly involved in a prior shooting, is identified as the driver in the fatal collision.
Sousa is hospitalized with serious injuries and will remain in custody upon medical clearance, according to Cranston police.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Steve Kerr apologized for commenting on Kristaps Porzingis' health, acknowledging he was not qualified to discuss the issue.
Porzingis has been diagnosed with postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, limiting him to 18 games this NBA season.
Porzingis was traded to Golden State, has played one game there, experiencing illness-related performance challenges.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are considering using draft picks and players like Mason Lohrei as trade chips before the March 6 NHL deadline.
Boston holds several first-round picks, including Toronto's 2026 selection, which might be valuable in trade negotiations.
Potential trade assets include young players like Matt Poitras and established players like Casey Mittelstadt and Viktor Arvidsson.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby, 29, could be traded this week; New England Patriots are among interested teams, says NFL insider Albert Breer.
Las Vegas Raiders reportedly receiving multiple calls about Crosby; no official trade discussions have taken place yet.
NFL free agency and trading period start March 11, raising possibility of Crosby trade as Raiders seek to maximize leverage.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq, 31, from Quincy, sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter of Barry Whelan in Boston.
Whelan died on March 20, 2023, after being assaulted on St. Patrick's Day; the death was ruled a homicide.
Incident followed a racial argument; Sadiq apologized for his actions during the sentencing hearing.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum remains sidelined with no official return date; Celtics ruled him out for Tuesday's game against the Bucks.
Bill Simmons suggests Tatum may make his return Friday at TD Garden against the Mavericks, but no confirmation yet.
Celtics are 40-20 this season without Tatum; ESPN reported he's cleared for 5-on-5 practice action.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court to decide legality of handgun carry permits for those under 21.
Mikai Thomson appeals conviction, arguing state law violating age restrictions is unconstitutional under Second Amendment.
State prosecutors argue age restrictions are lawful and necessary for public safety, citing historical precedent.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney prioritizes long-term roster strengthening over short-term gains before NHL trade deadline.
Boston seeks roster reinforcements but avoids rental market, focusing on players with long-term contracts.
Sweeney open to trading draft picks for impact players, but cautious about high acquisition costs.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo set to return against Celtics while Jaylen Brown is out with an illness
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is set to return from a right calf strain against the Celtics, after missing 15 games.
Jaylen Brown will miss the game against Milwaukee due to a non-COVID illness; Jayson Tatum also remains out.
The Celtics and Bucks have each won one game in their season series, with another matchup scheduled for April 3.

title: Citing ‘wokeness,’ Pentagon cancels military fellowships at New England schools like MIT and Tufts
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Pentagon canceled 93 fellowships at 22 institutions, including MIT, Tufts, and Yale, citing 'wokeness' concerns.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth criticized the schools for fostering 'anti-American resentment' and 'leftist ideology' in a recent memo.
New partner schools will be selected based on 'intellectual freedom' and minimal opposition to the Department of Defense policies.
